laws.title: Offences in relation to enemy and punishable with death
laws.description: Includes cowardice, assisting enemy or terrorists, spreading false alarms, abandoning post, or acts endangering mission success.
laws.simpleExplanation: If a person helps the enemy, behaves cowardly, or risks mission success during operations, they can face the death penalty.

laws.title: Offences in relation to enemy not punishable with death
laws.description: Covers being captured due to negligence, failing to rejoin service, or unauthorized communication with enemy.
laws.simpleExplanation: Negligence leading to capture or secret communication with the enemy can lead to imprisonment.

laws.title: Offences punishable more severely on active duty
laws.description: Includes misconduct like leaving post, sleeping on duty, spreading panic, or mishandling security protocols.
laws.simpleExplanation: Breaking discipline rules during active duty leads to harsher punishment.

punishments.description: Up to 14 years imprisonment on active duty.

laws.title: Mutiny
laws.description: Includes inciting, participating in, or failing to report mutiny.
laws.simpleExplanation: Rebellion against authority or not stopping/reporting it is severely punishable.

laws.title: Desertion and aiding desertion
laws.description: Covers desertion, harboring deserters, and failure to report desertion.
laws.simpleExplanation: Leaving duty or helping deserters is a serious offence.

punishments.description: Up to 7 years for harboring deserter.
Up to 2 years for failure to report.

laws.title: Absence without leave
laws.description: Covers unauthorized absence, overstaying leave, or failure to report for duty.
laws.simpleExplanation: Missing duty without permission can lead to punishment.

laws.title: Striking or threatening superior officer
laws.description: Includes assault, threats, or disrespectful language toward superior officers.
laws.simpleExplanation: Attacking or threatening a superior officer leads to severe punishment.

punishments.description: Up to 14 years on active duty or when officer is on duty.
Up to 5 years for insubordinate language.

laws.title: Disobedience to superior officer
laws.description: Covers willful disobedience of lawful commands.
laws.simpleExplanation: Ignoring or defying orders from a superior officer is punishable.

punishments.description: Up to 5 years when not on active duty.
